perturbation.original_text: Franchisee shall cause all Principal Owners, and their spouses, as of the Effective Date to execute and deliver to Pretzel Time concurrently with this Agreement, and all persons or entities which become Principal Owners, and their spouses, thereafter to execute and deliver to Pretzel Time promptly thereafter, the "Owner's and Guarantor's Undertaking and Assumption of Obligations," attached hereto as Exhibit D, or such other agreement as Pretzel Time prescribes from time to time, undertaking to be bound jointly and severally by, and to guarantee the payment and performance of, all provisions of this Agreement. Franchisee shall furnish to Pretzel Time, at any time upon request, in such form as Pretzel Time may require, a list of its shareholders or partners (of record and beneficially) reflecting their respective interests in Franchisee.
perturbation.changed_text: Franchisee shall cause all Principal Owners, and their spouses, as of the Effective Date to execute and deliver to Pretzel Time concurrently with this Agreement, and all persons or entities which become Unit Operators, and their spouses, thereafter to execute and deliver to Pretzel Time promptly thereafter, the "Owner's and Guarantor's Undertaking and Assumption of Obligations," attached hereto as Exhibit D, or such other agreement as Pretzel Time prescribes from time to time, undertaking to be bound jointly and severally by, and to guarantee the payment and performance of, all provisions of this Agreement. Franchisee shall furnish to Pretzel Time, at any time upon request, in such form as Pretzel Time may require, a list of its shareholders or partners (of record and beneficially) reflecting their respective interests in Franchisee.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Principal Owners' is being replaced with 'Unit Operators'. This creates confusion because 'Unit Operators' is not a defined term, and it's unclear if it has the same legal obligations as 'Principal Owners' as defined elsewhere in the contract, specifically exhibit B, where Principal Owners are specified. This change introduces uncertainty and contradiction, particularly when read in conjunction with sections referencing the obligations of Principal Owners. It is being contradicted in section 1.B where 'Principal Owner' is being defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Principal Owner" - Each Owner which: (1) is a general partner in Franchisee; or (2) has a direct or indirect equity interest: (a) in Franchisee of twenty percent (20%) or more (regardless of whether such Owner is entitled to vote thereon); or (b) in any Pretzel Time unit; or (3) is designated as a Principal Owner in Exhibit B of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Principal Owners' to 'Unit Operators' creates a contradiction because 'Principal Owner' is a defined term in the agreement, specifically in the contradicted text. By replacing it with 'Unit Operators', which is not a defined term, the agreement introduces ambiguity about who is subject to the 'Owner's and Guarantor's Undertaking and Assumption of Obligations'. This directly contradicts the definition of 'Principal Owner' and creates uncertainty about which individuals or entities are bound by the agreement, leading to a strong in-text contradiction under the Misaligned Terminology definition. This ambiguity would directly impact the enforceability of the guarantee obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Franchisee agrees that the Unit shall offer for sale all Products and no other products, which Pretzel Time, in its sole discretion, may authorize and/or require from time to time for the Unit. Franchisee agrees that the Unit shall not offer for sale or sell any Products or services at or from the Unit which have not been approved in writing by Pretzel Time or use the Site or Unit for any purpose other than the operation of a Pretzel Time Unit.
perturbation.changed_text: Franchisee agrees that the Unit shall offer for sale all Goods and no other products, which Pretzel Time, in its sole discretion, may authorize and/or require from time to time for the Unit. Franchisee agrees that the Unit shall not offer for sale or sell any Goods or services at or from the Unit which have not been approved in writing by Pretzel Time or use the Site or Unit for any purpose other than the operation of a Pretzel Time Unit.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Products' is being replaced with 'Goods.' While seemingly similar, 'Products' is a defined term in Section 1.B, outlining specifically what the Franchisee is allowed to sell. 'Goods' is not defined, creating ambiguity as to what the Franchisee is authorized to offer. It might be argued that goods may include items not specifically approved as Products under the terms of the franchise agreement. This contradicts section 1.B where Products are being defined and creates uncertainty in enforcing uniform offerings.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Products" - Products approved or required by Pretzel Time from time to time in its sole discretion for sale at or from Pretzel Time Units, including, without limitation, hand-rolled soft pretzels of various flavors including, without limitation, chocolate chip, raisin, honey-wheat, and cinnamon, frozen pretzels and other pretzel-related products and toppings, frozen yogurt, beverages, and other Pretzel Time-approved products, provided that the foregoing products are subject to modification or discontinuance in Pretzel Time's sole discretion from time to time and may include additional or substitute products.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the franchisee is allowed to sell 'Products', which are defined in the contradicted text as items approved by Pretzel Time. The changed text replaces 'Products' with 'Goods', which is undefined in the contract. This creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether 'Goods' encompasses only approved 'Products' or allows for a broader range of items, potentially conflicting with Pretzel Time's control over approved offerings as outlined in the definition of 'Products'. This ambiguity undermines the consistency and enforceability of the franchise agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Franchisee shall secure and maintain in force in his name all required licenses, permits, and certificates relating to the conduct of his business pursuant to this Agreement. Franchisee will conduct the Franchise in strict compliance with all applicable laws, ordinances, regulations, and other requirements of any federal, state, county, municipal or other government, including, without limitation, those laws and regulations pertaining to preparation, purchase and handling of food products, occupational hazards, health, safety and sanitation, worker's compensation insurance, unemployment insurance, and withholding and payment of all taxes.
perturbation.changed_text: Franchisee shall secure and maintain in force in his name all required licenses, permits, and permissions relating to the conduct of his business pursuant to this Agreement. Franchisee will conduct the Franchise in strict compliance with all applicable laws, ordinances, regulations, and other requirements of any federal, state, county, municipal or other government, including, without limitation, those laws and regulations pertaining to preparation, purchase and handling of food products, occupational hazards, health, safety and sanitation, worker's compensation insurance, unemployment insurance, and withholding and payment of all taxes.
perturbation.explanation: Here, changing "certificates" to "permissions" creates a contradiction. While 'licenses' and 'permits' are generally understood in a business context, the term 'permissions' is vague and undefined. The contract doesn't specify what 'permissions' are required, or from whom they need to be obtained, making it unenforceable. This creates an in-text contradiction because previously the certificates was understood for conducting the business.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Franchisee shall secure and maintain in force in his name all required licenses, permits, and permissions relating to the conduct of his business pursuant to this Agreement. Franchisee will conduct the Franchise in strict compliance with all applicable laws, ordinances, regulations, and other requirements of any federal, state, county, municipal or other government, including, without limitation, those laws and regulations pertaining to preparation, purchase and handling of food products, occupational hazards, health, safety and sanitation, worker's compensation insurance, unemployment insurance, and withholding and payment of all taxes.
perturbation.justification: The changed text and the contradicted text are identical. While the perturbation explanation argues that replacing 'certificates' with 'permissions' creates a contradiction because 'permissions' is vague, the contradicted text includes both terms. Since the contradicted text itself contains the allegedly problematic term, there is no contradiction introduced by the change as both texts are now identical. Although the explanation of the pertubation suggests a potential issue, the prompt requests an answer of NO because the change from certificates to permissions in one location and its resulting contradiction is not a strong contradiction.
